pages.raw_text:
P. III. Scullin, who came here from
Seattle in the interests of his arbitra-
ton scheme, was refused permission
to make an address before the trades
council. In explaining the refusal to
listen to Scullin Secretary Heskett
said:


Mr.. Scullin asked permission to
address the trades council in order
that he might make AN attack upon
State Labor Commissioners. Blackman.
and the trades council does not care
to listen to 1t. We do not favor the
plan of arbitration proposed by Mr..
Scullin."


Mr.. Scullin has been working for
some time to have Commissioner
Blackman removed from office. He
proposes an arbitration board of
which the mayor of the city where
the difficulty happens would take the
place at present filled by the state
commissioner.
